name: philosophy
description: A meta-plugin that deconstructs any prompt through philosophical lenses. Surfaces hidden assumptions, examines ethical dimensions, challenges positions through dialectical analysis, strips thinking to first principles, generates alternative framings, and examines purpose alignment. Usable standalone or as a reflective layer on any other plugin's output.

skills.name: phil_assumptions
skills.description: Surface hidden assumptions in any statement, plan, analysis, or decision. Makes visible the premises being taken for granted — especially the ones nobody thinks to question.

skills.name: phil_ethics
skills.description: Analyze ethical dimensions through multiple frameworks — consequentialist, deontological, virtue ethics, and justice. Identifies stakeholders, power asymmetries, and moral blind spots. Includes fiduciary awareness for regulated domains.

skills.name: phil_dialectic
skills.description: Thesis-antithesis-synthesis analysis. Constructs the strongest possible counter-argument to any position and explores what synthesis emerges from the tension.

skills.name: phil_first_principles
skills.description: Strip away all conventions, analogies, and inherited wisdom to examine what's actually known. Reconstruct from fundamentals alone and see what emerges.

skills.name: phil_reframe
skills.description: Generate radically different framings of a problem or situation. Each reframing changes what counts as the problem, what counts as a solution, and what counts as success.

skills.name: phil_purpose
skills.description: Examine whether stated purpose and actual function are aligned. Reveals the gap between what something claims to do and what it actually does.

skills.name: phil_cui_bono
skills.description: Who benefits? Traces the actual flow of benefits from a recommendation across financial, reputational, legal, data, and operational dimensions to determine whether the stated beneficiary is the actual beneficiary.

skills.name: phil_epistemology
skills.description: Audit evidence quality behind claims. When output says 'research shows' or 'data indicates' — what research? How strong? Correlational or causal? Single study or meta-analysis? Audits the knowledge claims themselves.

commands.name: phil_assumptions_check
commands.description: Quick Assumptions Check — 3-5 hidden assumptions with confidence ratings

commands.name: phil_reframe_quick
commands.description: Quick Reframe — 3 alternative framings in 2-3 sentences each

commands.name: phil_ethics_check
commands.description: Quick Ethics Check — who benefits, who's harmed, what's the key moral question

commands.name: phil_steelman
commands.description: Steelman — the strongest possible counter-argument to any position

commands.name: phil_purpose_check
commands.description: Quick Purpose Check — is stated purpose aligned with actual function?
